evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Establish and enforce best practices in testing, documentation, and data quality- making these part of the standard development lifecycle.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Establishing and enforcing testing and quality practices as a mandatory part of the development lifecycle foregrounds governance over shipping speed, matching rigour.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Core metrics like activation, engagement, and retention inform business decisions but carry no regulatory or financial-reporting consequence, matching moderate risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: Collaborate with Data Engineers on upstream data contracts and event schemas; ensuring consistent definitions across analytics, product, and AI use cases
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and evolve our semantic layer- creating a reliable abstraction over our data that enables consistent KPI definitions
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Building and evolving a semantic layer with upstream data contracts indicates an established, actively growing data stack, matching mid maturity.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build and evolve our semantic layer- creating a reliable abstraction over our data that enables consistent KPI definitions and supports downstream consumers, including LLM-based analytics agents.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The specific, detailed description of the semantic layer's purpose and downstream LLM consumers reflects concrete hiring-manager knowledge of the role, matching hiring_manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Define and implement core business metrics (e.g. activation, engagement, retention) as reusable, versioned data assets
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centers on building internal data assets (metrics, semantic layer) consumed across the company, matching internal_data orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own the transformation layer in dbt- design, build, and maintain modular, well-tested data models that define how data is structured and consumed across the company.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The verb 'own' applied to an entire transformation layer that defines company-wide data structure signals setting direction rather than executing scoped tasks, matching strategic autonomy.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Own the transformation layer in dbt- design, build, and maintain modular, well-tested data models
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The JD combines designing and building new models with maintaining the existing transformation layer, mixing new-build and maintain work, matching mixed.
evidence.ai_role: Build and evolve our semantic layer- creating a reliable abstraction over our data that enables consistent KPI definitions and supports downstream consumers, including LLM-based analytics agents.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Candidate builds semantic infrastructure that downstream AI systems (LLM agents) consume.
evidence.testing_framing: Establish and enforce best practices in testing, documentation, and data quality- making these part of the standard development lifecycle.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Candidate owns establishing and enforcing testing practices as mandatory development lifecycle requirement.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Build and evolve our semantic layer- creating a reliable abstraction over our data that enables consistent KPI definitions and supports downstream consumers, including LLM-based analytics agents.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Emphasizes operational reliability for downstream consumers and systems, with no compliance or regulatory framing.
